title: psilochenia bakeri subsp. cusickii
intro: Psilochenia bakeri subsp. cusickii, also known as the Cusick's psilochenia or Cusick's lewisia, is a plant species in the family Colchicaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of colchicine, a glycoside that has been used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as gout, arthritis, and skin irritations, and it has also been used to aid in wound healing and to reduce swelling.
